# Transcrição: 06--Me Too- vs. -Break-the-Ceiling- Products - Foundr Premium

- **Fonte:** 06--Me Too- vs. -Break-the-Ceiling- Products - Foundr Premium
- **Duração:** 06:59

- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:03]

So in this lesson we're going to look at Me Too products and also innovative what we call break the ceiling products. In the industry Me Too product is a commonly used terminology. It's something that you've seen happening in the market, you've seen a similar product selling to a similar market and you think I can do that as well or better versus a innovative break the ceiling product is a space or a problem in the market where you see I can solve that I can go and give these customers the solution they're looking for and it's going to propel your business probably further than a Me Too product. So now we're going to look at Me Too products and the thing about Me Too products is that there's some positive, easy wins that you can have. One of the good things with the Me Too product is you don't have to build the market, the market's already there, they're probably already buying a similar product. It mainly comes down to execution and branding and sometimes price depending what game you want to play. Now for us we've done Me Too products at Quadlock that are usually sort of cross sells or up sells so a good example is you come back to Quadlock to buy your new iPhone 11 case and we have iPhone 11 screen protectors. Now there's nothing really that different about our screen protector, it's just that you trust Quadlock, you're buying your case from Quadlock, you'll probably choose to buy your screen. You may choose to buy your screen protector from Quadlock. Now that means that screen protector for us is never going to be a hero product, we can't really grow the business that much off it, it's just a nice little upsell when they come back to buy something. We're never going to, Quadlock's never going to own the screen protector market but if you look at the active lifestyle iPhone mounting market where it's innovative and it's different, we sort of own that market and that's what we're known for and we happen to sell the Me Too screen protector on our website as well. Now the next type of product is what we call an innovative or break the ceiling product. Now this is very different and it can be harder to get off the ground but if you do make it work it will propel the business to a whole new level. Now for Quadlock our break the ceiling product or our innovative product is that of just our everyday smartphone mounting using the case and a mount. Now when we started that there wasn't really anything on the market like that so what that means is this gives us the ability to own that market but by owning that market it also means we have to build that market. So there was a problem people had, they didn't know the solution, we showed them solution and then we have to convince them this is how people want to do this. Now that can be a bit expensive at the start but in the end what can happen is and you can see on the screen now that during our journey Quadlock has actually become more of a popular search term than the search term iPhone bike mount and when you see the crossover

## [03:03 - 06:03]

there that means that our brand name is actually more popular in the search term so therefore we're owning that market, we've got protection in that market and it's very hard for competitors to come into that market and kick us off in a more position effectively. So now thinking about the pros and cons of both Me2 products and innovative break the ceiling products let's talk about how we can look at the market, look at what's going on around us and find opportunities within these markets. So what I like to do is look at the market for Me2 products sometimes and think what is something that we're already doing that we could leverage. So for us it comes back to the screen protectors we spoke about earlier whereas you can see that other people having success selling screen protectors it's a product that definitely your customer needs or wants or there's a portion of the market that you can go and attract with your messaging and your branding and sell the same thing to that existing market but hopefully better or alongside your other competitors. And then when it comes to looking at innovative break the ceiling products a thing I like to look at is once again is those growing markets new pain points that are coming into those markets but also other than just that it can be looking at where there's like a cross of technology. So on our very first product was called the Opener and it was iPhone bottle opener and what we thought we'd do is piggyback off the success of iPhones at the time you know that was when the smartphone market was really scaling up quite fast we did that in iPhone 4 and we thought iPhones are popular people like drinking beer it was a bit of a pain point you don't always have your keys on you to open up a beer bottle so we thought why don't we put a bottle opener on the back of an iPhone it's fatty it's sort of a bit of fun hadn't been done before and people might like that so we did that piggybacking off the iPhone popularity and straight away had a hit product so you know look around you see what other people are doing and you don't necessarily have to copy but you can take inspiration from that and try and apply it to something that you can put to the market that's new, innovative and when you get that product that's new and innovative it's really great place to be in for all the reasons we mentioned previously. In business sometimes you'll have the unique opportunities where you may have an opportunity to turn what could be a me too product into an innovative product or break the ceiling product and that is by taking advantage of maybe new trends and things like that that others won't have so being first and early in a part of the market and we did that with our car mount so we had still our original car mount was a bit innovative because it used the quad lock mechanism to mount your phone to your car mount but it was still just a car mount and it was an expensive car mount that so as a non-boarding product it was a little bit difficult for us but we did use it as a very competitive compelling upsell product along the way. When wireless charging came out we looked at that we thought here's

## [06:03 - 06:59]

an opportunity to do something new and different and what we did is we incorporated a wireless charging unit into our existing quad lock unit so therefore you had this really cool fast way of mounting your mobile phone to a car mount but that as soon as you had it mounted you had it also wirelessly charging so that took our existing car mount from a sort of me too style product into an innovative break the ceiling product and now we onboard people every day who had just come into us just to buy that car mount because of that wireless charging head so we were selling it for maybe a couple of years and it went from a me too into an innovative break the ceiling product and if you see that kind of opportunity I definitely jump on it. Okay so now what you need to do is decide whether your next product is going to be a me too product or an innovative break the ceiling product or mix and fill out the workbook attached and we'll catch you in the next lesson.
